# programs.powerline-go

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `programs.powerline-go`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `programs.powerline-go.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Powerline-go, a beautiful and useful low-latency prompt for your shell. |
| `programs.powerline-go.extraUpdatePS1` | `string` | Shell code to execute after the prompt is set. |
| `programs.powerline-go.modules` | `null or (list of string)` | List of module names to load. The list of all available modules as well as the choice of default ones are at https://github.com/justjanne/powerline-go . |
| `programs.powerline-go.modulesRight` | `null or (list of string)` | List of module names to load to be displayed on the right side. Currently not supported by bash. Specifying a value for this option will force powerline-go to use the eval format to set the prompt. |
| `programs.powerline-go.newline` | `boolean` | Set to true if the prompt should be on a line of its own. |
| `programs.powerline-go.package` | `package` | The powerline-go package to use. |
| `programs.powerline-go.pathAliases` | `null or (attribute set of string)` | Pairs of full-path and corresponding desired short name. You may use ‘~’ to represent your home directory but you should protect it to avoid shell substitution. |
| `programs.powerline-go.settings` | `attribute set of (boolean or signed integer or string or list of string)` | This can be any key/value pair as described in https://github.com/justjanne/powerline-go . |
